content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Closed the remaining final_review retry gap for transient availability-probe failures by treating `AppError::BackendUnavailable` as retryable when it carries transport-class metadata or conservative transient codex/openrouter probe text, so reviewer/arbiter per-member retry budget now covers `AgentExecutionService::check_availability` blips as well as invoke-time failures. Kept exhausted transient failures operator-readable by rewriting `BackendUnavailable` exhaustion into the existing final-review invocation error shape, and added focused regression coverage proving a reviewer availability probe failure retries, rechecks availability, and still records a single successful outer invocation.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the current final_review retry path and confirm the pending gap was transient `BackendUnavailable` errors from pre-invoke availability checks bypassing the new per-member retry classification.
2. [Completed] Extend transient codex failure classification and final_review exhaustion rewriting so availability-probe `BackendUnavailable` errors participate in the same retry/exhaustion flow as invoke-time transient failures.
3. [Completed] Add regression coverage for transient reviewer availability-probe failures and verify the full required repository gates on the amended tree.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub final_review_retries_transient_reviewer_availability_probe_failures -- --nocapture` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` passed.
- `nix build` passed on the current tree.

## Outstanding Risks

- There is still no dedicated arbiter-specific transient availability-probe regression; arbiter retries currently rely on the same shared final_review member retry path covered by reviewer tests.
